ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cells" (
  "cell_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cell */,
  "cell_name" TEXT /* Name of the cell (e.g., A1.B002777.3_39_F.1.1) */,
  "tissue" TEXT /* Tissue from which the cell was extracted */,
  "subtissue" TEXT /* Subtissue, if applicable */,
  "cell_ontology_class" TEXT /* Cell ontology class */,
  "cell_ontology_id" TEXT /* Cell ontology identifier */,
  "free_annotation" TEXT /* Free-form annotation for the cell */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Cluster identifier for the cell */,
  "mouse_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the mouse */,
  "tSNE_1" REAL /* t-SNE dimension 1 coordinate for visualization */,
  "tSNE_2" REAL /* t-SNE dimension 2 coordinate for visualization */,
  "plate_barcode" TEXT /* Barcode of the plate used in the experiment */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the experiment */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the sample */,
  "gene_expression_profile" TEXT /* Gene expression profile data */,
  "differential_expression_analysis" TEXT /* Results of differential expression analysis */,
  "cell_trajectory_analysis" TEXT /* Results of cell trajectory analysis */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the cell data was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the cell data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cell_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cells_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cells_cell_ontology_class FOREIGN KEY ("cell_ontology_class") REFERENCES cell_ontology ("ontology_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cells_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cells_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cells_tissue FOREIGN KEY ("tissue") REFERENCES tissues ("tissue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cells_mouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("mouse_id") REFERENCES mice ("mouse_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cells_plate_barcode FOREIGN KEY ("plate_barcode") REFERENCES plates ("plate_barcode")
)

CREATE TABLE "tissues" (
  "tissue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tissue */,
  "tissue_name" TEXT /* Name of the tissue */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the tissue */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the tissue data was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the tissue data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tissue_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cell_ontology" (
  "ontology_id" TEXT /* Ontology identifier for the cell class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the cell class */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cell class */,
  "parent_ontology_id" TEXT /* ID of the parent ontology class */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the ontology data was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the ontology data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ontology_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clusters" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cluster */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cluster */,
  "marker_genes" TEXT /* Marker genes for the cluster */,
  "cluster_size" INTEGER /* Number of cells in the cluster */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the cluster data was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the cluster data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mice" (
  "mouse_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the mouse */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the mouse */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the mouse */,
  "strain" TEXT /* Strain of the mouse */,
  "genotype" TEXT /* Genotype of the mouse */,
  "health_status" TEXT /* Health status of the mouse */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the mouse data was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the mouse data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "plates" (
  "plate_barcode" TEXT /* Barcode of the plate */,
  "experiment_date" TEXT /* Date when the experiment was conducted */,
  "experiment_type" TEXT /* Type of experiment */,
  "operator" TEXT /* Name or ID of the operator */,
  "lab_location" TEXT /* Location of the lab */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the plate data was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the plate data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("plate_barcode")
)

CREATE TABLE "experiments" (
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the experiment */,
  "experiment_name" TEXT /* Name of the experiment */,
  "experiment_type" TEXT /* Type of the experiment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the experiment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the experiment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the experiment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the sample */,
  "sample_name" TEXT /* Name of the sample */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the experiment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the sample data was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the sample data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table affected */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the record affected */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action was performed */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
